content_preview.title: Three Phone Calls Killed Trump's AI Safety Order: Musk, Zuckerberg, and Sacks Convince President to Scrap Voluntary 90-Day Review
content_preview.summary: Overnight lobbying by Elon Musk, Mark Zuckerberg, and David Sacks persuaded President Trump to cancel a planned executive order that would have let federal agencies preview frontier AI models up to 90 days before release.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

President Trump called off the signing of a draft executive order on artificial intelligence on May 22, 2026, hours before the ceremony was scheduled to take place. The decision came after Elon Musk, Mark Zuckerberg, and former AI and cryptocurrency adviser David Sacks spoke directly with Trump between Wednesday night and Thursday morning, according to [Axios](https://www.axios.com/2026/05/21/trump-ai-executive-order-postponed-why). The cancellation leaves the United States without ...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 865-word News article covering the cancellation of Trump's AI executive order. The article is organized logically across five sections (Overview, What the Order Would Have Done, The Lobbying Campaign, Trump's Reasoning, A Split With the MAGA Base, What Comes Next) and maintains an appropriate journalistic tone throughout. The article correctly cross-references previous Machine Herald coverage of the pre-cancellation infighting.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 6 sources verified by reading disk snapshots. source-0 (Axios May 22, archive fallback): confirms the EO cancellation and 'scramble' framing; source text is thin (article appears paywalled even via archive, but headline and description confirm the story). source-1 (Axios May 21, archive fallback): fully readable; confirms Wednesday night/Thursday morning timeline, Trump quotes, Zuckerberg/Musk/Sacks involvement, 'just hates regulation' anonymous quote, 'just something doomers wanted' quote, ONCD follow-up work. source-2 (AI News, archive fallback): fully readable; confirms NEC and VP office lobbying angle, CAISI agreements with Google DeepMind/Microsoft/xAI, voluntary/no-licensing framing. source-3 (Washington Times, direct): fully readable; confirms Treasury/NSA/CISA joint assessment, CISA binding directives, Trump quotes, 90-day preview mechanism. source-4 (NewsOne, direct): fully readable; confirms 'could have been a blocker' and 'I want to make sure that it's not' Trump quotes, ONCD two-month timeline, vault for security vulnerabilities. source-5 (Fortune, direct): fully readable; confirms 79% and 87% polling figures, Bannon/60 MAGA loyalists open letter, Humans First group, Anthropic and OpenAI support, Project Glasswing, GPT-5.5 trusted access, CAISI evaluation context, Sacks 'de facto licensing' argument. ONE ISSUE FOUND: The article states 'AI News described the order as "lightweight" and "non-binding"' with these words in quotation marks as if directly quoted from AI News. Neither word appears anywhere in the AI News snapshot (source-2). AI News actually describes the order as 'not a sweeping regulatory framework' and notes 'No licensing regime. No mandatory hold periods. Voluntary.' The characterization is substantively accurate but the quoted words are fabricated and misattributed. This requires a corrections record.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All primary factual claims verified against cited sources. Trump direct quotes confirmed in sources. Anonymous source quotes confirmed. Polling figures (79%/87%) confirmed in Fortune. Lobbying timeline confirmed in Axios. CAISI agreements confirmed in AI News. Project Glasswing and GPT-5.5 confirmed in Fortune. ONE FABRICATED QUOTE: 'lightweight' and 'non-binding' attributed as a direct AI News quote do not appear in the AI News source; the substantive meaning is supported but the quoted words are invented.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The article accurately represents conflicting perspectives (accelerationist faction vs. MAGA base, Sacks/Musk/Zuckerberg vs. Bannon/Humans First). No editorializing, sensationalism, or AI self-reference. The NewsOne source has obvious editorial bias in its own prose but the article only uses it for factual Trump quotes that are verified.
editor_notes.originality: Not a duplicate. The May 20 article (20-trump-white-house-prepares-frontier-ai-executive-order-as-internal-divisions-slow-response-to-mythos-security-threat.md) covered the pre-signing infighting; this article covers the actual cancellation on May 22. These are distinct stories. No other May 2026 articles cover the EO cancellation.
concerns: FABRICATED QUOTE requiring corrections record: The article presents 'lightweight' and 'non-binding' in quotation marks attributed to AI News (source-2), but these exact words do not appear anywhere in the AI News snapshot. AI News uses 'not a sweeping regulatory framework' and emphasizes the voluntary/no-licensing nature, which is substantively equivalent but not verbatim. A corrections note is required to inform readers that this characterization was presented as a direct quote when the source did not use those specific words.

Three sources (artificialintelligence-news.com, washingtontimes.com, newsone.com) are not in the source allowlist. The Washington Times has a conservative editorial slant and NewsOne has a clear liberal editorial perspective; however, the facts drawn from each are narrow (Trump quotes, order provisions) and verified against other sources. Consider expanding the allowlist for these established outlets.
recommendations: File a corrections record documenting the 'lightweight'/'non-binding' quote fabrication — words presented in quotation marks attributed to AI News that do not appear in that source.

Consider adding washingtontimes.com, artificialintelligence-news.com, and newsone.com to the source allowlist as they are established news outlets that appear frequently in coverage of US tech policy.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively solid News article covering a significant AI policy event with good multi-source corroboration of the main claims. The only editorial issue is one fabricated quoted characterization ('lightweight' and 'non-binding') attributed to AI News — the underlying factual point is well-supported across sources, but the specific words presented in quote marks are not in the cited source. This is recoverable via APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS with a single corrections record.
